steps.title: Identify the claim to support
steps.content: The question asks for the quotation that best supports the claim that the professor cannot adjust to life at the estate. The evidence must come from the professor's own words expressing explicit discomfort or rejection of estate life.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice by rule
steps.content:
Rule: the correct evidence must directly support the stated claim; a quotation that addresses a related but different idea does not count as support.
  • Choice A — 'Vanya says ... "A blank!"': Vanya's words express his own disillusionment with the professor's career — not the professor's inability to adjust to the estate. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Professor Serebrakoff ... "hateful to myself"': the professor expresses self-loathing in general, not specific maladjustment to the estate. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Professor Serebrakoff ... "unfamiliar with practical affairs"': the professor acknowledges unfamiliarity with practical matters, but says nothing about being unable to accept estate life. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Professor Serebrakoff ... "plunged into this wilderness ... condemned"': the professor directly contrasts his former comfortable life with being "condemned" to the estate, explicitly expressing his inability to accept country life. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
